title: Bavos-I heavy-duty military space platform

The '''Bavos-I heavy-duty military space platform''' was a type of [[Space station/Legends|space station]] that was designed by [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]] and utilized by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. Measuring 1,300 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length and crewed by a total of 800 personnel, the Bavos-I was outfitted with an armament of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]] and a [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] bank. [[Station 2LC/Blue]], an Imperial borderpost station in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds's]] [[Ringali Shell]], was a Bavos-I military [[Realspace/Legends|space]] platform.

==Description==
The Bavos-I heavy-duty military space platform was 1,300 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length and was produced by [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]]. A martial [[Space station/Legends|space station]], it was equipped with [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] and a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]]. Crewed by 600 personnel and 200 [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]], the Bavos-I could be kept running at a minimal crew of a hundred alongside twenty gunners. Additionally, the military [[Realspace/Legends|space]] station could house up to 200 additional personnel, which in some cases consisted of a hundred [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and a hundred [[Imperial Navy Trooper/Legends|Imperial Navy troopers]]. The Bavos-I had an armament that contained fifty medium [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]], which were each crewed by two gunners; and fifty additional anti-[[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] turbolasers, each also crewed by a pair of gunners; ten were situated to the right, left, back, and front, while the remaining ten were turrets. The military station also had a [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] bank, which contained thirty [[Missile/Legends|missiles]], and was fired from the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] by a single bridge [[Officer/Legends|officer]].<ref name="Orbit" />

The Bavos-I space station could carry fifty [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of cargo and was able to store enough consumables to last its crew three [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. However, the majority of these consumables, as well as other cargo and supplies, had to be stored outside the space station in [[Cargo pod/Legends|cargo pods]] that floated nearby. When supplies were needed, [[Cargo tug/Legends|cargo tugs]] had to go retrieve the pods. These cargo pods necessitated that up to a dozen cargo tugs be near the space station. In some cases, the Bavos-I had a complement of thirty-six [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and a pair of [[Troop transport/Legends|troop transports]].<ref name="Orbit" />

==History==
The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] utilized the Bavos-I by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. One such Bavos-I was [[Station 2LC/Blue]], an Imperial border station near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rhinnal/Legends|Rhinnal]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]' [[Ringali Shell]]. 2LC/Blue acted as a rescue outpost and an anti-[[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] staging area. By 0 ABY, Rendili StarDrive had produced a successor to the Bavos-I, the [[Bavos-II heavy-duty military space platform]], a more advanced version of the Bavos-I.<ref name="Orbit" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Bavos-I heavy-duty military space platform was first mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'', by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]]. The book was a supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and was a sequel of sorts to the previous publication, ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'', carrying on that book's theme by following the [[Privateer/Legends|privateering]] [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]''{{'s}} adventures. This article assumes that all Bavos-I space stations have cargo pods outside the space station like 2LC/Blue, the only [[canon]] example of a Bavos-I.<ref name="Orbit" />
